prestazioni: LS7 (7.0L GM small-block V8, engine code LS7) forced induction conversion using a Garrett GTX5533R Gen II turbocharger (1.35 A/R turbine housing, billet compressor wheel) with a custom 4-inch stainless steel turbo manifold by Dynatech, paired with a Tial MV-R 60mm external wastegate and a 44mm Tial Q blow-off valve for precise boost control at 22 psi on 100-octane fuel

Full dry-sump oiling system upgrade with a Peterson Fluid Systems 4-stage pump (model 424-STD) and a Moroso 8-quart aluminum oil tank (PN 22050), integrated with a Setrab 19-row oil cooler (PN 54019-1) and a custom billet aluminum oil pan by ARE Performance to eliminate oil starvation under sustained 1.5G lateral loads

Custom Motec M150 ECU with a bespoke Ultima RS-specific calibration map, featuring individual cylinder fuel trimming, traction control with 12-stage adjustability, and launch control optimized for a Tilton Super Starter (PN 54-1000) and a Sachs Performance twin-disc carbon clutch (PN 3000 950 095) with a 240mm flywheel for rapid gear engagement
estetica: Full carbon fiber widebody conversion using the Ultima RS-specific Rocket Bunny V3 aero kit (Pandem Design), including extended front fenders with integrated canards, rear over-fenders with a 50mm wider track, and a ducktail spoiler with a Gurney flap for increased downforce at 180 mph

Custom 3-piece forged aluminum wheels by HRE (model FF01, 19x12 front and 20x13 rear) with a 5x120 bolt pattern, wrapped in Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 R tires (285/30 ZR19 front, 335/30 ZR20 rear) and paired with AP Racing CP9460-360 6-piston front and CP9460-320 4-piston rear calipers with 380mm and 355mm two-piece rotors, respectively

Full interior overhaul with a custom roll cage by Safety Devices (model SDR-RS-01, FIA-spec 8-point with integrated door bars), Sparco Pro 2000 seats (PN 01939NR) with 6-point Schroth Profi II-6 harnesses, and a MOMO Mod.78 steering wheel with a quick-release hub, all trimmed in Alcantara with red stitching and carbon fiber door cards by Status Racing
